title: Analog front end non-linear model estimation
abstract: Certain aspects of the present disclosure provide techniques for analog front end (AFE) non-linear model estimation. An example method includes transmitting a first message including a request for a second wireless communications device to transmit a signal to the first wireless communications device, receiving, from the second wireless communications device, the signal based on the first message, determining a non-linear model of an AFE of the first wireless communications device based on the signal received from the second wireless communications device, and taking one or more actions based on the determined non-linear model of the AFE.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for wireless communication by a first wireless communications device, comprising: transmitting a first message including a request for a second wireless communications device to transmit a signal to the first wireless communications device; receiving, from the second wireless communications device, the signal based on the first message; determining a non-linear model of an analog front end (AFE) of the first wireless communications device based on the signal received from the second wireless communications device; and taking one or more actions based on the determined non-linear model of the AFE. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the signal comprises at least one of a signal that has a peak to average power ratio (PAPR) equal to or exceeding a threshold PAPR or a signal that has a predefined amplitude distribution. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein: the signal comprises a pre-configured payload; and the signal is received in one of a control message or a data message. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the signal comprises one of a reference signal, a synchronization signal block (SSB), or a sounding reference signal (SRS). 5. The method of claim 1, wherein: the first message further includes one or more parameters for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the signal to the first wireless communications device; and the signal is received in accordance with the one or more parameters. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the one or more parameters include a non-linear (NL) transmission (TX) floor for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the signal to the first wireless communications device. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the NL TX floor is indicated as an error vector magnitude (EVM). 8. The method of claim 5, wherein: the one or more parameters include a transmission configuration indicator (TCI) state for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the signal to the first wireless communications device; and the TCI state is associated with a transmit beam of the second wireless communications device and a corresponding receive beam of the first wireless communications device. 9. The method of claim 5, wherein the one or more parameters include at least one of: a bandwidth for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the signal to the first wireless communications device; a carrier frequency for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the signal to the first wireless communications device; or a transmission power for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the signal to the first wireless communications device. 10. The method of claim 1, further comprising receiving, from the second wireless communications device, an indication of a non-linear (NL) transmission (TX) floor or an error vector magnitude (EVM) associated with the signal received from the second wireless communications device. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the NL TX floor comprises an actual NL TX floor used to transmit the signal. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein: the first message further includes a request for the actual NL TX floor used to transmit the signal; and receiving the actual NL TX floor used to transmit the signal is based on the request for the actual NL TX floor used to transmit the signal. 13. The method of claim 1, wherein taking the one or more actions comprises: determining a correction model based on the determined non-linear model; and linearizing a non-linear response of the AFE based on the determined correction model. 14. The method of claim 1, further comprising receiving one or more cross link interference (CLI) reports from one or more neighboring wireless communications devices, including the second wireless communications device. 15. The method of claim 14, further comprising transmitting the one or more CLI reports to a network entity, wherein: the one or more CLI reports include at least one CLI report associated with the second wireless communications device; and receiving the signal from the second wireless communications device is based on the one or more CLI reports transmitted to the network entity, including the at least one CLI report associated with the second wireless communications device. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein: the signal comprises a sounding reference signal (SRS); and the method further comprises receiving, from the network entity, an indication of time-frequency resources for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the SRS and an indication of a non-linear (NL) transmission (TX) floor for the SRS. 17. The method of claim 14, further comprising: determining, based on the one or more CLI reports, a set of neighboring wireless communications devices, from the one or more neighboring wireless communications devices, that have a sufficient link budget for transmitting the signal to the first wireless communications device; and selecting the second wireless communications device from the set of neighboring wireless communications devices. 18. The method of claim 17, further comprising storing parameters associated with the received one or more CLI reports, wherein selecting the second wireless 

communications device is based on the stored parameters associated with a CLI report, of the one or more CLI reports, received from the second wireless communications device. 19. The method of claim 17, further comprising receiving and storing an indication of a respective non-linear (NL) transmission (TX) floor for each of the neighboring wireless communications devices, wherein selecting the second wireless communications device is further based on a respective NL TX floor associated with the second wireless communications device. 20. The method of claim 17, wherein: transmitting the first message including the request comprises transmitting the first message to a network entity; the request in the first message requests that the network entity allocate timefrequency resources for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit a sounding reference signal (SRS); and the first message further includes one or more parameters for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the SRS to the first wireless communications device. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the one or more parameters include at least one of: a non-linear (NL) transmission (TX) floor for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the SRS to the first wireless communications device; a transmission configuration indicator (TCI) state for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the SRS to the first wireless communications device; a bandwidth for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the SRS to the first wireless communications device; a carrier frequency for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the SRS to the first wireless communications device; or a transmission power for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the SRS to the first wireless communications device. 22. The method of claim 20, wherein receiving the signal comprises receiving the SRS from the second wireless communications device in accordance with the one or more parameters. 23. The method of claim 1, wherein: transmitting the first message including the request comprises transmitting the first message including the request to the second wireless communications device using a sidelink channel; and receiving the signal from the second wireless communications device comprises receiving the signal from the second wireless communications device using the sidelink channel. 24. An apparatus for wireless communication by a first wireless communications device, comprising: one or more memories comprising executable instructions; and one or more processors configured to execute the executable instructions and cause the first wireless communications device to: transmit a first message including a request for a second wireless communications device to transmit a signal to the first wireless communications device; receive, from the second wireless communications device, the signal based on the first message; determine a non-linear model of an analog front end (AFE) of the first wireless communications device based on the signal received from the second wireless communications device; and take one or more actions based on the determined non-linear model of the AFE. 25. The apparatus of claim 24, wherein: the first message further includes one or more parameters for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the signal to the first wireless communications device; and the signal is received in accordance with the one or more parameters. 26. The apparatus of claim 25, wherein the one or more parameters include at least one of: a non-linear (NL) transmission (TX) floor for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the SRS to the first wireless communications device; a transmission configuration indicator (TCI) state for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the SRS to the first wireless communications device; a bandwidth for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the SRS to the first wireless communications device; a carrier frequency for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the SRS to the first wireless communications device; or a transmission power for the second wireless communications device to use to transmit the SRS to the first wireless communications device. 27. The apparatus of claim 24, wherein, in order to take the one or more actions, the one or more processors are further configured to cause the first wireless communications device to: determine a correction model based on the determined non-linear model; and linearize a non-linear response of the AFE based on the determined correction model. 28. The apparatus of claim 24, wherein the one or more processors are further configured to cause the first wireless communications device to: receive one or more cross link interference (CLI) reports from one or more neighboring wireless communications devices, including the second wireless communications device; determine, based on the one or more CLI reports, a set of neighboring wireless communications devices, from the one or more neighboring wireless communications devices, that have a sufficient link budget for transmitting the signal to the first wireless communications device; and select the second wireless communications device from the set of neighboring wireless communications devices. 29. An apparatus for wireless communication by a first wireless communications device, comprising: means for transmitting a first message including a request for a second wireless communications device to transmit a signal to the first wireless communications device; means for receiving, from the second wireless communications device, the signal based on the first message; means for determining a non-linear model of an analog front end (AFE) of the first wireless communications device based on the signal received from the second wireless communications device; and means for taking one or more actions based on the determined non-linear model of the AFE. 30. A non-transitory computer readable medium for wireless communication by a first wireless communications device, comprising: instructions that, when executed by one or more processors of a first wireless communications device, cause the first wireless communications device to: transmit a first message including a request for a second wireless communications device to transmit a signal to the first wireless communications device; receive, from the second wireless communications device, the signal based on the first message; determine a non-linear model of an analog front end (AFE) of the first wireless communications device based on the signal received from the second wireless communications device; and take one or more actions based on the determined non-linear model of the AFE.
